title: uvariopsis dioica
intro: Uvariopsis dioica, also known as the Okamba or African walnut, is a plant species in the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in African medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The health benefits of Uvariopsis dioica include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and providing relief from fever and other symptoms of malaria. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, with herbal preparations often involving the root bark and seeds.
